questions.explanation: Bare-metal means the program runs directly on the MCU/SoC hardware (typically with startup code and a linker script) without an OS kernel.

options.A: Application code running directly on hardware without an operating system
options.B: Application code running inside a virtual machine on Linux
options.C: Application code that only uses high-level scripting languages
options.D: Application code that runs only when connected to the internet

questions.question: In embedded systems, what best describes a 'bare-metal' application?

questions.explanation: An AND gate produces a HIGH output only if every input is HIGH.
questions.question: Which digital logic gate outputs HIGH only when all inputs are HIGH?

questions.explanation: Git is the standard distributed version control system used to track code changes and collaborate.
questions.question: Which tool is most commonly used for distributed version control in software configuration management?

questions.explanation: UART is an asynchronous serial interface commonly used for console logs and device-to-device serial links.
questions.question: Which interface is typically used for simple point-to-point asynchronous serial communication (TX/RX) between two devices?

questions.explanation: A periodic timer interrupt is commonly used as a system tick for scheduling, timeouts, and periodic task execution.

options.A: Run long blocking computations to maximize CPU utilization
options.B: Implement a periodic system tick to schedule time-based tasks
options.C: Poll SPI in a tight loop to avoid using DMA
options.D: Disable all other interrupts to ensure deterministic logging

questions.question: A microcontroller timer is configured to generate an interrupt every 1 ms. What is the most appropriate use of this interrupt in a real-time firmware design?

questions.explanation: The device tree describes the board hardware so the Linux kernel can discover devices and match them to drivers.

options.A: Provide the compiler with CPU instruction scheduling hints
options.B: Describe hardware (addresses, interrupts, buses) so the kernel can bind drivers without hardcoding board details
options.C: Encrypt firmware images for secure boot
options.D: Replace the bootloader by loading the kernel directly from flash

questions.question: In embedded Linux, what is the primary role of a device tree (DTS/DTB)?

questions.explanation: ISRs typically 'give' a binary semaphore or use task notifications/event flags to wake a task safely and efficiently.

options.B: Binary semaphore (or direct-to-task notification/event flag equivalent)
options.D: Busy-wait loop in the ISR until the task runs

questions.question: In FreeRTOS or Zephyr, which synchronization primitive is best suited to signal an event from an ISR to a task with minimal overhead?

questions.explanation: With compiler optimizations, functions may be inlined or removed, causing breakpoints on the original symbol to not correspond to executed instructions.

options.A: The function was inlined or optimized away, so the expected symbol/address is not executed as a distinct call site
options.B: SWD cannot support breakpoints; only JTAG can
options.C: GDB cannot debug C code, only assembly
options.D: OpenOCD requires the UART baud rate to match the CPU clock

questions.question: You are debugging a Cortex-M target via SWD using OpenOCD and GDB. You set a breakpoint at a function, but it never triggers even though the function is executed. Which is the most likely cause in an optimized build?

questions.explanation: LTO and dead-code elimination can remove unused functions/data and improve inlining decisions, often reducing flash and sometimes RAM (e.g., fewer globals).

options.A: Enable link-time optimization (LTO) and remove unused code paths (dead-code elimination)
options.B: Replace all fixed-size buffers with larger dynamic allocations to avoid stack usage
options.C: Turn off compiler warnings to reduce binary size
options.D: Add extensive printf logging to help diagnose memory usage

questions.question: A bare-metal firmware must fit into 64 KB flash and 8 KB RAM. Which change is most likely to reduce both flash and RAM usage without changing functionality?

questions.explanation: Logic programming expresses requirements as constraints; a solver searches for satisfying assignments, enabling automatic generation of configuration/code from the solved model.

options.A: Write a C function with nested if/else statements and manually test all combinations
options.B: Model the constraints as facts/rules and use a solver (e.g., Prolog/SMT) to find a valid pin assignment and generate code from the solution
options.C: Use a faster compiler optimization level to automatically infer pin modes
options.D: Store the constraints in a README and rely on code review to enforce them

questions.question: You want to auto-generate a correct-by-construction GPIO configuration routine from a set of constraints: 'Pins P1 and P2 must be outputs, P3 must be input with pull-up, and no two peripherals may claim the same pin.' Which approach best matches 'logic programming' used for automatic programming here?
